danger_signals.visual_form: Sprite-based adversaries from Fearsome Five and hired criminals
danger_signals.meaning: Active threats in stage areas; defeat required to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual attack patterns from enemies and bosses
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate danger requiring avoidance or cape deflection

danger_signals.visual_form: Entry to stage finale areas housing criminal bosses
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated threat level; significant combat encounter ahead

danger_signals.visual_form: Heart icon in top-left HUD decreasing
danger_signals.meaning: Player vulnerability state; loss of reserve capacity

danger_signals.visual_form: Final stage environment following criminal stage completion

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible gas canisters (Thunder, Heavy, Arrow variants)
safety_signals.meaning: Resource replenishment; enablement of special weapon functionality

safety_signals.visual_form: Transition screen after criminal boss defeat
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety; progression to next challenge tier

safety_signals.visual_form: Standard weapon always accessible via Select button
safety_signals.meaning: Constant defensive capability; no ammunition dependency

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: St. Canard city setting as jurisdiction

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Criminal organization name; Floating Fortress as base
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Steelbeak as primary operative; Fearsome Five as hired agents
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: F.O.W.L.'s Floating Fortress as final stage environment

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Individual criminal identities: The Liquidator, Megavolt, Dr. Reginald Bushroot, Quackerjack, Professor Moliarty, Wolfduck
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each criminal appears as distinct boss sprite with unique attack patterns
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Six distinct stage environments corresponding to criminal territories

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Crime wave active in St. Canard
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy presence throughout accessible stages; criminal bosses occupying stage environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hostile NPC behavior; boss combat initiation upon stage completion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player character equipped with Gas Gun and cape
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can engage enemies, collect power-ups, and navigate platforming challenges

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage selection screen shows completed (cleared) and available stages
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New stage tier becomes accessible after three criminal defeats; progression toward Steelbeak

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: F.O.W.L.'s Floating Fortress becomes accessible final stage after all six criminals defeated
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Steelbeak confrontation becomes possible; final boss battle mechanics activate

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Special gas canister collectibles scattered throughout stages; limited capacity indicator
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must choose which special gas type to carry; Switch via Select button required

environmental_storytelling.example: Six distinct stage environments correspond to hired criminals' characteristics (implied through stage progression structure); each represents different area of St. Canard under criminal control
F.O.W.L.'s Floating Fortress final stage represents command center; spatial design indicates escalation from street-level crime to organized crime infrastructure
Darkwing begins with standard Gas Gun only; access to Thunder, Heavy, and Arrow gas variants acquired through stage progression indicates increasing capability to counter organized threat
Sewer environment in stage design indicates urban underworld locations; unused smiley-face placeholder suggests environmental detail development for criminal hideout areas

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can jump, move laterally, fire Gas Gun, deflect projectiles with cape, switch between weapon types
Patrol stage areas and attack with projectiles; require defeat to clear stage
Unique attack patterns per criminal; defeat triggers stage completion and progression to next tier
Appears only in F.O.W.L.'s Floating Fortress after all six criminals defeated; represents culmination of threat
Remain stationary until acquired; restore health or provide ammunition for special weapons

notes: Darkwing Duck is based on Mega Man 5 architecture, employing stage-select gameplay where player chooses from available criminal stages. The game communicates world-state primarily through stage progression rather than dynamic environmental changes. Faction identity is conveyed through character opposition (S.H.U.S.H. implied as Darkwing's employer vs. F.O.W.L./Fearsome Five as antagonists). Danger and safety signals are mechanical/UI-based rather than subtle environmental storytelling. The game uses binary safety states: in-stage (danger) and between-stage (neutral). No subtle faction visual markers or color coding documented in available sources.
sources_used: tcrf - Darkwing Duck (NES) - The Cutting Room Floor
